info.title: Deferno API
info.description: Task-management API for the Deferno SaaS. Every response is wrapped in the v0.1 envelope: `{ "version": "0.1", "data": <payload> }` on success, or `{ "version": "0.1", "error": { "code": "snake_case", "message": "..." } }` on failure. The `version` field is pinned at `0.1`; a bump is a breaking change for every client. Native iOS/Android clients authenticate with a personal API token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`) created via `POST /auth/tokens`.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's profile (id, username, display name, role, personal-org id and slug).

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's display name and/or username. A username change is subject to a 30-day cooldown (returns 429 inside the window).

get.description: Fetch the authenticated user's UI settings (done-visibility windows, theme, timezone, analytics + drag-and-drop toggles). An absent stored blob yields defaults.

patch.description: Partially update the authenticated user's UI settings. Omitted fields are left unchanged; an explicit null clears a nullable field (visibility window or timezone). Visibility seconds and the IANA timezone are validated.

400.description: Out-of-range visibility window or invalid IANA timezone

post.summary: Sign in and start a session
post.description: Exchange login_name + password (+ the in-flight `auth_request`) for either a completed OIDC callback URL or an MFA-challenge requirement. Public endpoint; rate-limited per normalized login_name.

200.description: Callback URL, or an MFA challenge requirement

400.description: Missing auth_request or bad credentials shape

get.description: List the caller's API tokens. Returns metadata only — never the secret token material.

post.description: Create a personal API token. The raw `token` is returned exactly once — store it securely. This is the bearer credential native mobile clients send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

200.description: Token created; raw token shown once

delete.description: Revoke (delete) an API token by id.

post.description: Create a recurring chore in the caller's personal org. The response wraps the new chore in an ItemEnvelope with the `ref`, `org_slug`, `sequence`, and `type` identity fields.

400.description: Invalid recurrence rule

delete.description: Archive (soft-delete) a chore and clean up its canonical-ref bindings. Idempotent: archiving an already-archived chore still succeeds.

patch.description: Partially update a chore. Omitted fields are left unchanged. For a recurring chore, `recurring_scope` ('this'/'following'/'all') plus `recurrence_id` selects which instances the edit applies to; a 'following' edit forks a new chore linked via predecessor/successor.

200.description: Updated chore (or the going-forward chore when scope is 'following')

400.description: Invalid recurrence scope/rule or missing recurrence_id

post.description: Resolve the chore's earliest unresolved occurrence (scheduled/missed/in-progress) with the given action, advancing the parent's next due date on completion.

404.description: Chore not found, or no unresolved occurrence

get.description: List a chore's occurrences within a date window. Each entry carries a derived status (scheduled/missed/in-progress/done-on-time/done-late/skipped) merged from stored records and the recurrence schedule.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive start of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

200.description: Derived chore occurrence views in the window

put.description: Set a chore occurrence's status (in-progress / done / skipped) for a scheduled date. Marking done advances the parent's next due date. On a terminal mark with non-terminal subtasks, returns 409 unless `cascade_subtasks=true`.

409.description: Non-terminal subtasks exist; pass cascade_subtasks=true to override

delete.description: Clear a chore occurrence record for a specific date.

post.summary: Reschedule a chore occurrence (not yet implemented)
post.description: Reschedule a chore occurrence to a new date. Not yet implemented — chores still use the legacy occurrence storage path; the endpoint is registered but returns 501 until that migration lands.

501.description: Chore reschedule requires the v0.2 unified-occurrence storage migration

delete.description: Soft-delete a comment (Task-embedded, legacy-table, or occurrence-embedded). Returns the resulting tombstone with `deleted_at` set and no body. Auth mirrors parent-item edit auth.

403.description: Caller is neither owner nor assignee of the parent item

404.description: Comment, item, or occurrence not found

patch.description: Edit a comment's body and/or privacy. Resolves the comment whether it lives on a Task or on a recurring item's occurrence. Auth mirrors parent-item edit auth (owner or assignee).

403.description: Caller is neither owner nor assignee of the parent item

404.description: Comment, item, or occurrence not found

422.description: Nothing to update, empty/over-long body, or the comment is deleted

post.description: Create an event (optionally recurring) in the caller's personal org. The response wraps the new event in an ItemEnvelope with the `ref`, `org_slug`, `sequence`, and `type` identity fields.

400.description: Invalid recurrence rule or end_time before complete_by

delete.description: Archive (soft-delete) an event and clean up its canonical-ref bindings. External (synced) events reject archival.

403.description: Event is external (synced); deletion is blocked

patch.description: Partially update an event. Omitted fields are left unchanged. For a recurring event, `recurring_scope` ('this'/'following'/'all') plus `recurrence_id` selects which instances the edit applies to; a 'following' edit forks a new event linked via predecessor/successor. External (synced) events reject intrinsic-field edits.

200.description: Updated event (or the going-forward event when scope is 'following')

400.description: Invalid recurrence scope/rule, missing recurrence_id, or status 'in-review' (not allowed on events)

403.description: Event is external (synced); intrinsic-field edits are blocked

get.description: List an event's stored occurrences (unified shape with per-occurrence status, overrides, comments, attachments, and reschedule markers) within a date window.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive start of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

post.description: Set an event occurrence's status (in-progress / done / dropped) for a scheduled date. Events never produce 'done-late'. A terminal mark advances or clears the parent's complete_by; with non-terminal subtasks, returns 409 unless `cascade_subtasks=true`.

400.description: Invalid status for an event (e.g. done-late)

409.description: Non-terminal subtasks exist; pass cascade_subtasks=true to override

delete.description: Delete an event occurrence record for a specific date.

get.description: List an Event occurrence's attachments. S3-provider records carry a freshly-signed GET URL; URL-provider records pass the stored link through.

400.description: Parent item is not an Event

post.summary: Add attachments to an event occurrence
post.description: Commit presigned S3 uploads and/or add URL-provider attachments to an Event occurrence. Returns the full post-commit attachment list with freshly-signed GET URLs for S3 entries.

400.description: No intents or urls supplied, cap exceeded, parent is not an Event, or an intent is invalid

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Event is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

415.description: A URL attachment's MIME type is blocked

post.summary: Presign uploads for an event occurrence
post.description: Batch-presign S3 PUT URLs for files to attach to a specific Event occurrence. The response is returned unwrapped (no envelope).

400.description: Attachments cap exceeded, parent is not an Event, or an invalid file descriptor

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Event is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

413.description: A file exceeds the per-attachment size limit

415.description: A file's MIME type is blocked

delete.description: Remove an attachment from an Event occurrence and best-effort delete the underlying S3 object. Returns 204 No Content.

400.description: Parent item is not an Event

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Event is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

404.description: Event, occurrence, or attachment not found

patch.summary: Edit an event occurrence attachment caption
patch.description: Set, change, or clear (caption: null) the caption on an Event occurrence attachment. The caption field is required; omitting it is a 422.

400.description: Caption failed validation (empty or too long), or parent is not an Event

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Event is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

404.description: Event, occurrence, or attachment not found

422.description: Caption field omitted (use null to clear)

post.summary: Add a comment to an event occurrence
post.description: Add a comment to a specific Event occurrence. The occurrence row is created on demand if it does not exist yet. Private comments are encrypted under the author's key.

400.description: Parent item is not an Event

403.description: Caller is neither owner nor assignee of the Event

delete.description: Delete the first comment on an Event occurrence. Returns the resulting tombstone with `deleted_at` set and no body. Auth mirrors Event edit auth.

400.description: Parent item is not an Event

403.description: Caller is neither owner nor assignee of the Event

404.description: Event, occurrence, or comment not found

patch.description: Edit the first comment on an Event occurrence (body and/or privacy). Auth mirrors Event edit auth.

400.description: Parent item is not an Event

403.description: Caller is neither owner nor assignee of the Event

404.description: Event, occurrence, or comment not found

422.description: Nothing to update, empty/over-long body, or the comment is deleted

post.description: Move a single event occurrence to a new date without touching the recurrence rule. The origin date is marked Dropped with `rescheduled_to`; a fresh Scheduled occurrence lands on the target date with `rescheduled_from`. Returns the target-date occurrence.

200.description: The freshly-created occurrence on the target date

400.description: new_date must differ from the origin date

post.description: Submit a feedback report (bug, feature request, or general). Optionally references previously-presigned attachment ids, each with an optional caption.

400.description: Subject/body too long, invalid category, or an invalid attachment caption/intent

post.description: Batch-presign S3 PUT URLs for feedback attachments. The browser uploads to each URL, then references the returned ids when submitting feedback. The response is returned unwrapped (no envelope).

400.description: An invalid file descriptor

413.description: A file exceeds the per-attachment size limit

415.description: A file's MIME type is blocked

post.description: Create a recurring habit in the caller's personal org. The response wraps the new habit in an ItemEnvelope with the `ref`, `org_slug`, `sequence`, and `type` identity fields.

400.description: Invalid recurrence rule

delete.description: Archive (soft-delete) a habit and clean up its canonical-ref bindings. Idempotent: archiving an already-archived habit still succeeds.

patch.description: Partially update a habit. Omitted fields are left unchanged. For a recurring habit, `recurring_scope` ('this'/'following'/'all') plus `recurrence_id` selects which instances the edit applies to; a 'following' edit forks a new habit linked via predecessor/successor.

200.description: Updated habit (or the going-forward habit when scope is 'following')

400.description: Invalid recurrence scope/rule, missing recurrence_id, or status 'in-review' (not allowed on habits)

get.description: List a habit's recorded occurrences (check-ins) within a date window.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive start of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end of the occurrence date window (YYYY-MM-DD).

post.summary: Mark or clear a habit occurrence
post.description: Mark or clear a habit occurrence (check-in) for a date (defaults to today). On mark-done with non-terminal subtasks, returns 409 unless `cascade_subtasks=true`.

409.description: Non-terminal subtasks exist; pass cascade_subtasks=true to override

delete.description: Clear a habit occurrence (check-in) for a specific date.

post.summary: Reschedule a habit occurrence (not yet implemented)
post.description: Reschedule a habit occurrence to a new date. Not yet implemented — habits still use the legacy occurrence storage path; the endpoint is registered but returns 501 until that migration lands.

501.description: Habit reschedule requires the v0.2 unified-occurrence storage migration

get.description: List every non-deleted item (Task/Habit/Chore/Event) in the caller's personal org as a polymorphic JSON array. Supports OData-style `$filter`/`$orderby`/`$select`/`$top`/`$skip` query params plus a `window=all` flag; each element carries a `kind` discriminator and the `ref`/`org_slug`/`sequence`/`type` identity fields.

200.description: Polymorphic item objects (v0.1 envelope around a freeform array)

400.description: Malformed `$query`/`$filter`, or a filter references a non-filterable field

get.summary: Resolve an imported alias to an item
get.description: Resolve an imported-system alias (e.g. `ABC-223`, `octo/repo#44`, `cal_789`) to its polymorphic item within the caller's personal org. Aliases are case-insensitive.

400.description: Alias must not be empty

get.summary: Resolve a canonical ref to an item
get.description: Resolve a canonical cross-org ref (`<org_slug>-<sequence>`, e.g. `acme-1842`) to its polymorphic item. Authorization gates on the resolved org; a non-member sees the same 404 as an unknown ref.

400.description: Malformed canonical ref

get.summary: Resolve a sequence shorthand to an item
get.description: Resolve an in-context shorthand sequence to its polymorphic item within the caller's personal org. Accepts a bare integer (`1842`) or the shorthand form (`#1842`, which may arrive URL-encoded as `%231842`).

400.description: Not a numeric sequence or shorthand

get.description: Fetch a single item by UUID as a polymorphic view (Task/Habit/Chore/Event), wrapped in an ItemEnvelope carrying the `ref`/`org_slug`/`sequence`/`type` identity fields. The `kind` discriminator selects the variant.

delete.description: Soft-delete an item by UUID, dispatching on its kind (Task/Habit/Chore/Event), and clean up its canonical-ref bindings.

post.summary: Convert an item to another kind
post.description: Convert an item from its current kind into `to` (Task/Habit/Chore/Event), preserving its id. `complete_by` is required for Event/Chore/Habit; `recurrence` is additionally required for Chore/Habit (optional for Event); `end_time` is Event-only. Converting to the current kind is idempotent and returns 200; a real conversion returns 201. The response is the converted polymorphic view.

400.description: Invalid conversion (missing/invalid complete_by, recurrence, or end_time)

get.description: Return an item's action/history list, resolving the item's kind (Task/Habit/Chore/Event) from the index and reading that entity's `actions`. A missing entity yields an empty list; an id not owned by the caller yields 404.

post.description: Set or clear the pinned flag on any item (Task/Habit/Chore/Event), dispatching on its kind via the index. Body is `{ "pinned": bool }`. Keeps the user's pinned list authoritative across all sibling kinds.

get.description: List the caller's saved searches (named pointers to a `/tasks` URL query string) in display order.

post.description: Create a saved search from a name and a raw URL query string. Rejects an empty/over-long name and enforces the per-user saved-search limit.

400.description: Name empty/too long, or saved-search limit reached

post.description: Reorder the caller's saved searches. The body's `ids` must include every saved-search id exactly once (no missing ids, no duplicates).

400.description: Ids must include every saved search exactly once (missing or duplicate ids)

delete.description: Delete a saved search by id.

patch.description: Update a saved search's name and/or query string. Omitted fields are left unchanged.

400.description: Name empty/too long

get.description: List every non-deleted task in the caller's personal org. Each element is an ItemEnvelope carrying identity fields plus descendant-progress counts.

post.description: Create a task in the caller's personal org. The response wraps the new task in an ItemEnvelope, adding the `ref`, `org_slug`, `sequence`, and `type` identity fields.

403.description: Parent is an external (synced) item; child-attach is blocked

get.description: List calendar events in a half-open [start, end) local-date window: recurring instances, one-off items due in range, and synced external events. Times are projected to UTC.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description:
IANA timezone the window is interpreted in (defaults to the
user's stored timezone, then UTC).

400.description: Invalid start or end date, or invalid IANA timezone

get.description: List mood-vector readings captured on the caller's finished tasks, for plotting mood/productivity trends over time.

List the caller's pinned tasks in pin order. Each element wraps a PinnedTaskResponse (task summary plus optional label) in an ItemEnvelope carrying the `ref`/`org_slug`/`sequence`/`type` identity fields.

post.description: Reorder the caller's pinned tasks. The body's `task_ids` must be an exact permutation of the current pinned set (no missing ids, no duplicates).

400.description: Not an exact permutation of the pinned set, or duplicate ids

patch.summary: Set or clear a pinned task's label
patch.description: Set or clear the optional display label on a pinned task. An absent/null `label` clears it.

get.description: Fetch the curated daily plan (Task view) for a date. Auto-seeds from recurring items and carries forward unfinished items. The date defaults to today in the caller's timezone.

400.description: Invalid date format or IANA timezone

post.summary: Add a task to the daily plan
post.description: Add a task to the daily plan for a date (defaults to today in the caller's timezone). Returns 204 No Content.

400.description: Invalid date format or IANA timezone

post.summary: Remove a task from the daily plan
post.description: Remove a task from the daily plan for a date (defaults to today in the caller's timezone). Returns 204 No Content.

400.description: Invalid date format or IANA timezone

post.description: Replace the daily plan's task ordering for a date with the supplied id list (defaults to today in the caller's timezone). Returns 204 No Content.

400.description: Invalid date format or IANA timezone

get.description: Full-text search across the caller's tasks (query must be at least 2 characters), with optional status, label, date-range, and parent filters.

400.description: Query too short

get.description: List the caller's actionable tasks for today, each scored and annotated with an urgency reason, wrapped with item-identity fields.
Fetch a single task by UUID.

delete.description: Soft-delete a task and clean up its canonical-ref bindings.

patch.description: Partially update a task. Omitted fields are left unchanged; an explicit null clears a nullable field. For a recurring task, `recurring_scope` (this/following/all) plus `recurrence_id` selects which instances the edit applies to.

400.description: Invalid recurrence scope, or completing a task with active children

403.description: Task is external (synced); intrinsic-field edits are blocked

get.description: List a Task's attachments. S3-provider records carry a freshly-signed GET URL (5-minute TTL); URL-provider records pass the stored link through.

400.description: Parent item is not a Task

post.description: Commit previously-presigned S3 uploads and/or add URL-provider attachments to a Task. Returns the full post-commit attachment list; S3 entries carry a freshly-signed GET URL.

400.description: No intents or urls supplied, cap exceeded, parent is not a Task, or an intent is invalid

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Task is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

415.description: A URL attachment's MIME type is blocked

post.description: Batch-presign S3 PUT URLs for files to attach to a Task. The browser uploads to each URL, then calls the commit endpoint. The response is returned unwrapped (no envelope).

400.description: Attachments cap exceeded, parent is not a Task, or an invalid file descriptor

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Task is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

413.description: A file exceeds the per-attachment size limit

415.description: A file's MIME type is blocked

delete.description: Remove an attachment from a Task and best-effort delete the underlying S3 object. Returns 204 No Content.

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Task is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

patch.description: Set, change, or clear (caption: null) the caption on a Task attachment. The caption field is required; omitting it is a 422.

400.description: Caption failed validation (empty or too long), or parent is not a Task

403.description: Caller lacks edit auth, the Task is external (read-only), or the feature is disabled

422.description: Caption field omitted (use null to clear)

get.summary: List a task or item's comments
get.description: List a task's Activity-timeline comments. For a Task these are item-level comments; for a recurring kind they are the union of every occurrence's comments, each tagged with its occurrence date. Comments the caller cannot decrypt are returned without a body.

post.summary: Add a comment to a task or item
post.description: Add a comment to a task or item. For a recurring kind (Chore, Habit, or Event) the comment lands on the current actionable occurrence — the local date of the item's `complete_by` cursor — falling back to the most-recent occurrence when the series is exhausted. Private comments are encrypted under the author's key.

422.description: Empty or over-long body, or no actionable occurrence to comment on

ApiTokenView.description: Safe-to-return view of an API token (no key material).

Attachment.description: v0.2 Attachment record. See module docs for compatibility rules.

caption.description: Optional user-supplied caption; `null` when none has been set.

caption_updated_at.description:
When the caption was last changed. Equals `created_at` when the
caption was set at upload and never edited; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

caption_updated_by.description:
Id of the user who last changed the caption; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

created_at.description: When the attachment record was created.

created_by.description: Id of the user who created the attachment.

reference.description:
Provider-specific reference: for `S3`, the bucket-relative object
key; for `Url`, the user-supplied URL string.

AttachmentPresignBatchResponse.description:
Response to an attachment presign request: one entry per file in the
batch, each carrying the new attachment id, a presigned PUT URL, and
the URL's expiry.

AttachmentProvider.description:
Storage provider for an attachment: `S3` for files uploaded through
presign, `Url` for user-supplied links.

AttachmentView.description:
Read-side wire shape returned by the attachment list and commit
responses. For `S3` records, `url` is a freshly-signed GET URL; for
`Url` records, `url` is the user-supplied URL as-is.

caption.description: Optional user-supplied caption; `null` when none has been set.

caption_updated_at.description:
When the caption was last changed. Equals `created_at` when the
caption was set at upload and never edited; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

caption_updated_by.description:
Id of the user who last changed the caption; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

console_url.description:
Zitadel admin console URL (derived from ZITADEL_API_URL). Present
when Zitadel integration is configured; the webui shows the
"Admin Console" link iff `is_admin && console_url`.

is_admin.description:
True iff the signed-in user holds the Zitadel application-role
identified by DEFERNO_ADMIN_ROLE_KEY (an "IdP admin"). Drives the
"Admin Console" affordance only — never routing. Distinct from
`role: Admin`, which is Deferno's username-derived app-admin
(gates the in-app /admin view).

org_slug.description:
Immutable, URL-safe slug for the user's personal org. Used by the
webui to build canonical `/o/{slug}/items/{seq}` URLs and to render
`acme-1842` style refs without an extra round trip. Empty for
unmigrated rows (`refs_v1` and `users_workspace_key_v1` backfill).
Populated by the `/api/auth/me` handler from the `Org` row.
(Field was named `workspace_key` pre-rename; `#[serde(alias)]`
preserves wire-format compatibility with clients still sending the
old name.)

personal_org_id.description:
The user's personal-org id. Equal to `id` for solo users; differs
when the user belongs to a real org. The webui uses the (in)equality
to decide whether to show owner/assignee fields on item detail views.

username_change_available_at.description:
When the user is allowed to change their username again, if they're
currently inside the 30-day cooldown window. `None` when no cooldown
is active (never edited, or cooldown already elapsed). The UI uses
this to disable the input proactively rather than relying solely on
the 429 response from `PATCH /me`.

oneOf.description:
FREQ=MONTHLY. `interval` is the cycle (1=every month, 2=every other,
etc.). `on` selects the day within the cycle.

FREQ=YEARLY. `interval` is the cycle (1=annual, 2=biennial, etc.).
`month` is 1..=12 and `day` is 1..=31.

Fallback for RFC 5545 patterns that aren't first-class above —
preserves the raw RRULE so no data is lost during import. The
recurrence expander treats this as "unknown cadence" (no
occurrence expansion until a follow-up adds support).

Cadence.description:
The repeating *cadence* of a recurrence — the "every day / every Tuesday /
the 15th" pattern (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly, etc.), independent of
any end bound. The optional end lives on the wrapping [`Recurrence`] struct.

CadenceMode.description:
How a chore's next scheduled date advances after an occurrence is
completed or dropped. `Rolling` advances relative to when the
occurrence was closed out; `Fixed` advances on the fixed recurrence
schedule regardless of when it was closed. Defaults to `Rolling`.

CalendarEvent.description:
A calendar event — either a recurring instance from defernodate, a
one-off Deferno item with a `complete_by` date, or an event synced
from an external source (Google Calendar today; more in the future).

all_day.description: Whether the event is rendered as an all-day chip.

calendar_color.description: Hex color string for the source calendar, when known.

calendar_name.description: Display name of the source calendar (e.g. "Personal" / "Work").

external_url.description:
External link for synced events (e.g. Google's `htmlLink`).
Absent for Deferno-owned items.

oneOf.description: Local-only annotations (notes, labels) attached to a synced event.

source.description:
Source of the event. Defaults to `"deferno"` for items the user
owns inside Deferno. Synced Google events are tagged
`"google_calendar"`.

CalendarEventOverlay.description:
Local-only annotation a user can attach to a synced external event.
Mirrors `google_calendar::EventOverlay` but lives in `models` so the
public API surface doesn't import the integration module directly.

cadence_mode.description:
How the chore's next scheduled date advances after an occurrence is
completed or dropped. Defaults to `Rolling`.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`
(e.g. `"21:00"`). `null` falls back to the end of the user's day.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for chores imported from external systems. `None` for
Deferno-native rows. See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this chore was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this chore. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

Comment.description:
A user-owned comment on a task.

The body is encrypted at rest. Two key choices:
- `is_private == true`  → encrypted with the author's user-DEK
- `is_private == false` → encrypted with the owning org's org-DEK

`body_enc` is the storage shape: an AEAD ciphertext with AAD binding the
blob to `(comment_id, "body")`. It is `None` only on soft-delete
tombstones.

`body` is a transient plaintext field populated by the handler / exporter
after a successful decrypt with the appropriate key. It is never stored —
`#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` keeps it out of the
wire shape when absent. A non-creator reading a private comment will see
`body_enc` carrying ciphertext they can't decrypt and `body == None`.

This field separation is a deliberate restoration of the design from
commit `df91d7b` (Apr 20, 2026) that was rolled back in `660f0752`.
`body_enc` mirrors the original field name.

CommentListResponse.description:
Shape returned by `GET /tasks/{id}/comments` and `GET /items/{id}/comments`.

Entries are [`TimelineComment`]s: for a Task they are item-level comments
(no occurrence date); for a recurring kind they are the union of every
occurrence's comments, each tagged with its occurrence date.

CommitAttachmentsPayload.description:
Request body to commit attachments to a task. Mixes previously-presigned
S3 uploads and direct URL-provider links; at least one must be present.

intents.description:
Entries returned by a prior presign call, each single-use. Each may
carry an optional `caption` stamped on the attachment at commit time.

urls.description: Url-provider entries — committed directly with no upload.

ConvertItemPayload.description:
Body for `POST /items/:id/convert`.

Converts the addressed item from its current `ItemKind` into `to`.
`complete_by` is required when the destination is Event/Chore/Habit;
`recurrence` is additionally required for Chore/Habit (and optional
for Event). `end_time` is Event-only and validated against
`complete_by` at the handler boundary. Requesting `to` equal to the
current kind is allowed (idempotent — returns the current shape).

end_time.description: Event-only. Optional. Must be `>= complete_by` when present.

oneOf.description:
Required when `to = Habit | Chore`; optional for Event; ignored
when `to = Task` (Tasks are non-recurring by definition).

How the chore's schedule advances after an occurrence is completed
or dropped. Omit to default to `Rolling`.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`.
Omit for the end of the user's day.

subtask_template.description:
Optional ordered checklist of subtasks attached to every occurrence.
Omit (or send `[]`) for none.

CreateCommentPayload.description:
Body for POST /tasks/{task_id}/comments.

The frontend sends plaintext; the handler encrypts with the creator's DEK
before persistence (mirroring task title/description encryption).

subtask_template.description:
Optional ordered checklist of subtasks attached to every occurrence.
Omit (or send `[]`) for none.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`.
Omit for the end of the user's day.

subtask_template.description:
Optional ordered checklist of subtasks attached to every occurrence.
Omit (or send `[]`) for none.

DerivedChoreOccurrenceStatus.description:
Virtual statuses derived at read time from a ChoreOccurrence record's
absence or presence relative to today.

EncryptedBlob.description:
An AEAD ciphertext serialised as two url-safe base64 strings.

Stored as strings so the existing JSON wire format stays human-inspectable
and keeps round-tripping through `serde_json` without binary-blob gymnastics.

ct.description: Base64 (url-safe, no padding) of `ciphertext || 16-byte GCM tag`.

nonce.description: Base64 (url-safe, no padding) of the 12-byte nonce.

additionalProperties.description:
Optional structured detail beyond `code` and `message` (for
example `limit_bytes` or `hint`). Flattened directly under
`error`, so these machine-readable fields sit alongside `code`
and `message`.

data.description:
Read-side wire shape returned by the attachment list and commit
responses. For `S3` records, `url` is a freshly-signed GET URL; for
`Url` records, `url` is the user-supplied URL as-is.

caption.description: Optional user-supplied caption; `null` when none has been set.

caption_updated_at.description:
When the caption was last changed. Equals `created_at` when the
caption was set at upload and never edited; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

caption_updated_by.description:
Id of the user who last changed the caption; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

console_url.description:
Zitadel admin console URL (derived from ZITADEL_API_URL). Present
when Zitadel integration is configured; the webui shows the
"Admin Console" link iff `is_admin && console_url`.

is_admin.description:
True iff the signed-in user holds the Zitadel application-role
identified by DEFERNO_ADMIN_ROLE_KEY (an "IdP admin"). Drives the
"Admin Console" affordance only — never routing. Distinct from
`role: Admin`, which is Deferno's username-derived app-admin
(gates the in-app /admin view).

org_slug.description:
Immutable, URL-safe slug for the user's personal org. Used by the
webui to build canonical `/o/{slug}/items/{seq}` URLs and to render
`acme-1842` style refs without an extra round trip. Empty for
unmigrated rows (`refs_v1` and `users_workspace_key_v1` backfill).
Populated by the `/api/auth/me` handler from the `Org` row.
(Field was named `workspace_key` pre-rename; `#[serde(alias)]`
preserves wire-format compatibility with clients still sending the
old name.)

personal_org_id.description:
The user's personal-org id. Equal to `id` for solo users; differs
when the user belongs to a real org. The webui uses the (in)equality
to decide whether to show owner/assignee fields on item detail views.

username_change_available_at.description:
When the user is allowed to change their username again, if they're
currently inside the 30-day cooldown window. `None` when no cooldown
is active (never edited, or cooldown already elapsed). The UI uses
this to disable the input proactively rather than relying solely on
the 429 response from `PATCH /me`.

cadence_mode.description:
How the chore's next scheduled date advances after an occurrence is
completed or dropped. Defaults to `Rolling`.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`
(e.g. `"21:00"`). `null` falls back to the end of the user's day.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for chores imported from external systems. `None` for
Deferno-native rows. See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this chore was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this chore. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

data.description:
A user-owned comment on a task.

The body is encrypted at rest. Two key choices:
- `is_private == true`  → encrypted with the author's user-DEK
- `is_private == false` → encrypted with the owning org's org-DEK

`body_enc` is the storage shape: an AEAD ciphertext with AAD binding the
blob to `(comment_id, "body")`. It is `None` only on soft-delete
tombstones.

`body` is a transient plaintext field populated by the handler / exporter
after a successful decrypt with the appropriate key. It is never stored —
`#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` keeps it out of the
wire shape when absent. A non-creator reading a private comment will see
`body_enc` carrying ciphertext they can't decrypt and `body == None`.

This field separation is a deliberate restoration of the design from
commit `df91d7b` (Apr 20, 2026) that was rolled back in `660f0752`.
`body_enc` mirrors the original field name.

Shape returned by `GET /tasks/{id}/comments` and `GET /items/{id}/comments`.

Entries are [`TimelineComment`]s: for a Task they are item-level comments
(no occurrence date); for a recurring kind they are the union of every
occurrence's comments, each tagged with its occurrence date.

all_day.description:
Whether the event is all-day in the originating provider (no
start/end clock time, only a date). Defaults to `false` for
Deferno-native and legacy rows.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for events imported from external calendars
(Google/Apple/Proton/Microsoft). `None` for Deferno-native rows.
See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this event was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this event. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`
(e.g. `"21:00"`). `null` falls back to the end of the user's day.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for habits imported from external trackers (Habitify,
Streaks, etc.). `None` for Deferno-native rows. See
`ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this habit was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this habit. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

How the chore's next scheduled date advances after an occurrence is
completed or dropped. Defaults to `Rolling`.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`

Provenance for chores imported from external systems. `None` for
Deferno-native rows. See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

predecessor_id.description:

this chore. Empty when there are none, and always present on the

data.description:
Generic per-item response envelope. `#[serde(flatten)]` merges the
inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire
shape is a flat object (matching the spec).

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no
sequence is bound — that case is structurally impossible after
`refs_v1` has run (the boot migration is unconditional), but legacy
rows mid-rollout or a transient lookup failure could produce one.
Emitting `{ ref: "acme-0", sequence: 0 }` would be a wire-level lie;
dropping the fields lets the webui's existing optional handling
(see `webui/src/types/index.ts`) fall back to UUID routes.

all_day.description:
Whether the event is all-day in the originating provider (no
start/end clock time, only a date). Defaults to `false` for
Deferno-native and legacy rows.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for events imported from external calendars
(Google/Apple/Proton/Microsoft). `None` for Deferno-native rows.
See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this event was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this event. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no

attachments.description: Attachments embedded on this task; empty when there are none.

comment.description: Embedded comment on this task; empty when there are none.

descendant_done.description:
Computed: number of non-deleted descendants that are done.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

descendant_total.description:
Computed: total number of non-deleted descendants.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for tasks imported from external systems (Google Tasks,
Todoist, etc.). `None` for Deferno-native rows. See
`ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

finished_at.description:
Timestamp when the task entered a terminal state (Done or Dropped).
Set on Open→Done, Open→Dropped, and InProgress→Dropped. Preserved
across Done→Dropped (does not lose the original Done time).
Cleared on any transition back to Open / InProgress / InReview.
Used by the visibility-window filter and by `mood_history` (which
gates on `status == Done` before reading this).

group_id.description:
Optional group ownership. Always None today — reserved for the
future Groups subsystem (Spec 2). Handlers ignore this field.

occurrence_id.description:
Set when this task is a subtask materialized from a recurring item's
occurrence; `null` for ordinary tasks. Removed together with that
occurrence.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

series_id.description:
ID of the defernodate Series backing a recurring sibling that shares
this Task's UUID. Tasks themselves are non-recurring (recurrence
lives on Habit/Chore/Event), but this pointer is preserved across
the Task ↔ sibling boundary so series-aware reads still resolve.

subtask_template_id.description:
Set when this task was materialized from a recurring item's subtask
template; holds that template entry's id. `null` for ordinary tasks
and for ad-hoc subtasks.

data.description:
Generic per-item response envelope. `#[serde(flatten)]` merges the
inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire
shape is a flat object (matching the spec).

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no
sequence is bound — that case is structurally impossible after
`refs_v1` has run (the boot migration is unconditional), but legacy
rows mid-rollout or a transient lookup failure could produce one.
Emitting `{ ref: "acme-0", sequence: 0 }` would be a wire-level lie;
dropping the fields lets the webui's existing optional handling
(see `webui/src/types/index.ts`) fall back to UUID routes.

A single dated occurrence of a recurring item (chore, habit, or event).

The optional `*_override` fields let a client tailor one scheduled date
without altering the parent item. When an override is set (`Some`), it
shadows the parent item's matching descriptive field for that single
occurrence; when `null`, the value falls through to the parent. Only
descriptive fields support overrides — structural fields such as `id`,
`parent_id`, and `status` always come from the occurrence or its parent.

rescheduled_from.description:
Set on the occurrence that lands on the target date after a
reschedule, carrying the origin date the occurrence moved from.
Pairs with `rescheduled_to` on the origin date so a client can
render a "moved from" affordance.

rescheduled_to.description:
Set on the origin occurrence after a reschedule: the origin date's
record is marked dropped and points to the new date the occurrence
moved to. Pairs with `rescheduled_from` on the target date so a
client can render a "moved to" affordance.

data.description:
A user's saved search — a named pointer to a `/tasks` URL query string.

Stored as a JSON-encoded `Vec<SavedSearch>` under
`user:{user_id}:saved_searches` (mirrors pinned-tasks storage).

query_string.description: Raw URL query string (without leading "?"), e.g. "kinds=task&statuses=open".

oneOf.description:
The user has at least one Deferno-challengeable second factor; the
password-only session is stashed server-side (`mfa_pending`) and the
client must complete one of `factor_types` via the /challenge endpoints.
We do NOT complete the OIDC login here — that would bypass MFA.

attachments.description: Attachments embedded on this task; empty when there are none.

comment.description: Embedded comment on this task; empty when there are none.

descendant_done.description:
Computed: number of non-deleted descendants that are done.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

descendant_total.description:
Computed: total number of non-deleted descendants.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for tasks imported from external systems (Google Tasks,
Todoist, etc.). `None` for Deferno-native rows. See
`ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

finished_at.description:
Timestamp when the task entered a terminal state (Done or Dropped).
Set on Open→Done, Open→Dropped, and InProgress→Dropped. Preserved
across Done→Dropped (does not lose the original Done time).
Cleared on any transition back to Open / InProgress / InReview.
Used by the visibility-window filter and by `mood_history` (which
gates on `status == Done` before reading this).

group_id.description:
Optional group ownership. Always None today — reserved for the
future Groups subsystem (Spec 2). Handlers ignore this field.

occurrence_id.description:
Set when this task is a subtask materialized from a recurring item's
occurrence; `null` for ordinary tasks. Removed together with that
occurrence.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

series_id.description:
ID of the defernodate Series backing a recurring sibling that shares
this Task's UUID. Tasks themselves are non-recurring (recurrence
lives on Habit/Chore/Event), but this pointer is preserved across
the Task ↔ sibling boundary so series-aware reads still resolve.

subtask_template_id.description:
Set when this task was materialized from a recurring item's subtask
template; holds that template entry's id. `null` for ordinary tasks
and for ad-hoc subtasks.

dashboard_done_visibility_seconds.description: Same idea, applied client-side on the Dashboard.

drag_and_drop_enabled.description:
Experimental: when true, the webui renders the task-card drag handle
and the dnd-kit reorder/reparent surface. Default `false` because
the surface is still maturing and we don't want to expose it to
users by default. Backward-compat: pre-shipping blobs lack the key,
so `serde(default = "...")` populates `false` instead of failing.

global_done_visibility_seconds.description:
Seconds past `finished_at` (tasks) or `complete_by` (one-off events)
after which a non-recurring item is hidden from default lists.
`None` ⇒ never hide.

theme_family.description:
Visual theme family. `serde(default)` + lenient deserializer so a
missing or unknown stored value does not poison the whole blob.

time_zone.description:
IANA timezone name (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles"). `None` ⇒ UTC.
Used to compute "today" / day windows for daily-plan and calendar
endpoints. Per-request `?tz=` query parameter overrides this.

tracking_enabled.description:
Whether the webui captures behavioural analytics for this user.
Default `true` (opt-in). Backward-compat: blobs from before this
field shipped have no key, so `serde(default = "...")` populates
`true` rather than failing.

items.description: Safe-to-return view of an API token (no key material).

Read-side wire shape returned by the attachment list and commit
responses. For `S3` records, `url` is a freshly-signed GET URL; for
`Url` records, `url` is the user-supplied URL as-is.

caption.description: Optional user-supplied caption; `null` when none has been set.

caption_updated_at.description:
When the caption was last changed. Equals `created_at` when the
caption was set at upload and never edited; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

caption_updated_by.description:
Id of the user who last changed the caption; `null` when no caption
has ever been set.

items.description:
A calendar event — either a recurring instance from defernodate, a
one-off Deferno item with a `complete_by` date, or an event synced
from an external source (Google Calendar today; more in the future).

all_day.description: Whether the event is rendered as an all-day chip.

calendar_color.description: Hex color string for the source calendar, when known.

calendar_name.description: Display name of the source calendar (e.g. "Personal" / "Work").

external_url.description:
External link for synced events (e.g. Google's `htmlLink`).
Absent for Deferno-owned items.

oneOf.description: Local-only annotations (notes, labels) attached to a synced event.

source.description:
Source of the event. Defaults to `"deferno"` for items the user
owns inside Deferno. Synced Google events are tagged
`"google_calendar"`.

items.description:
Generic per-item response envelope. `#[serde(flatten)]` merges the
inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire
shape is a flat object (matching the spec).

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no
sequence is bound — that case is structurally impossible after
`refs_v1` has run (the boot migration is unconditional), but legacy
rows mid-rollout or a transient lookup failure could produce one.
Emitting `{ ref: "acme-0", sequence: 0 }` would be a wire-level lie;
dropping the fields lets the webui's existing optional handling
(see `webui/src/types/index.ts`) fall back to UUID routes.

inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire
shape is a flat object (matching the spec).

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no
sequence is bound — that case is structurally impossible after
`refs_v1` has run (the boot migration is unconditional), but legacy
rows mid-rollout or a transient lookup failure could produce one.
Emitting `{ ref: "acme-0", sequence: 0 }` would be a wire-level lie;
dropping the fields lets the webui's existing optional handling

allOf.description:
Lightweight task summary for list endpoints — omits description,
actions history, mood vectors, and other fields that are only
useful when viewing a single task in detail.

items.description:
Generic per-item response envelope. `#[serde(flatten)]` merges the
inner item's fields next to the ref/identifier fields so the wire
shape is a flat object (matching the spec).

`canonical_ref` and `sequence` are skipped from the JSON when no
sequence is bound — that case is structurally impossible after
`refs_v1` has run (the boot migration is unconditional), but legacy
rows mid-rollout or a transient lookup failure could produce one.
Emitting `{ ref: "acme-0", sequence: 0 }` would be a wire-level lie;
dropping the fields lets the webui's existing optional handling
(see `webui/src/types/index.ts`) fall back to UUID routes.

The optional `*_override` fields let a client tailor one scheduled date
without altering the parent item. When an override is set (`Some`), it
shadows the parent item's matching descriptive field for that single
occurrence; when `null`, the value falls through to the parent. Only

rescheduled_from.description:
Set on the occurrence that lands on the target date after a
reschedule, carrying the origin date the occurrence moved from.
Pairs with `rescheduled_to` on the origin date so a client can
render a "moved from" affordance.

rescheduled_to.description:

record is marked dropped and points to the new date the occurrence
moved to. Pairs with `rescheduled_from` on the target date so a

items.description:
A user's saved search — a named pointer to a `/tasks` URL query string.

Stored as a JSON-encoded `Vec<SavedSearch>` under
`user:{user_id}:saved_searches` (mirrors pinned-tasks storage).

query_string.description: Raw URL query string (without leading "?"), e.g. "kinds=task&statuses=open".

ErrorEnvelope.description:
OpenAPI-only view of the error envelope (`{ "version": "0.1", "error": {…} }`).

The live code emits errors through `Envelope<T>` with `data: None`; this
dedicated, non-generic struct gives the generated spec — and the
Swift/Kotlin clients built from it — a single named error type to decode
into. It is referenced as the `default` response on every documented
operation. Kept in sync with [`Envelope`] by construction: both serialize
`version` + `error` identically.

all_day.description:
Whether the event is all-day in the originating provider (no
start/end clock time, only a date). Defaults to `false` for
Deferno-native and legacy rows.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for events imported from external calendars
(Google/Apple/Proton/Microsoft). `None` for Deferno-native rows.
See `ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this event was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this event. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

ExternalProvenance.description:
Source-agnostic provenance for an entity that was imported from an
external system. Embedded on Task / Habit / Event / Chore as
`external: Option<ExternalProvenance>`. `None` ⇒ Deferno-native row.

`source` is freeform so the same shape carries data from Google
Calendar, Apple Calendar (CalDAV), Proton Calendar, Microsoft Graph,
Google Tasks, Habitify, etc. Conventions: `"google_calendar"`,
`"apple_calendar"`, `"proton_calendar"`, `"microsoft_calendar"`.

id.description:
Provider-specific composite identifier. Format depends on the
source — Google Calendar uses `"{calendar_id}:{event_id}"`; iCal
providers typically use the iCal UID. Treat as opaque.

master_id.description:
For modified instances of a recurring series, the provider's
pointer to the master record. `None` for one-off or master rows.

source.description: Short, stable provider name. See struct doc for conventions.

updated_at.description:
Provider's last-modified timestamp. Used by reconciliation to
detect upstream changes between syncs.

url.description:
Provider-side view URL (e.g. Google `htmlLink`). Optional —
CalDAV/EWS sources may not surface a user-facing URL.

FeedbackPresignBatchResponse.description:
Schema is renamed `FeedbackPresignBatchResponse` in the OpenAPI
components to avoid colliding with the structurally-distinct
attachment presign response
(`handlers::task_attachments::PresignBatchResponse`).

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Optional per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`
(e.g. `"21:00"`). `null` falls back to the end of the user's day.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for habits imported from external trackers (Habitify,
Streaks, etc.). `None` for Deferno-native rows. See
`ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

predecessor_id.description:
Set when this habit was produced by splitting a recurring series
with "this and following"; holds the id of the entity the split
branched off from. `null` when it never resulted from a split.

subtask_template.description:
Ordered checklist of subtasks materialized onto each occurrence of
this habit. Empty when there are none, and always present on the
wire (serialized as `[]`, never omitted).

IntentEntry.description:
A single attachment-commit entry. Accepts either a bare attachment-id
string or an object `{ "id": "...", "caption": "..." }` carrying an
optional caption stamped on the attachment at commit time.

ItemListResponse.description:
OpenAPI shape for `GET /items`: the v0.1 envelope around a freeform array
of item objects. The element shape varies with OData `$select` projection,
so elements are documented as free-form objects rather than a fixed schema.

oneOf.description: "The 15th of every month" — `day` is 1..=31.

"The second Wednesday of every month" — `nth` is 1..=5 or `-1`
(last). Mirrors RFC 5545 `BYDAY=2WE`.

MonthlyAnchor.description: Anchors the Monthly recurrence within the month. See `Cadence::Monthly`.

Occurrence.description:
A single dated occurrence of a recurring item (chore, habit, or event).

The optional `*_override` fields let a client tailor one scheduled date
without altering the parent item. When an override is set (`Some`), it
shadows the parent item's matching descriptive field for that single
occurrence; when `null`, the value falls through to the parent. Only
descriptive fields support overrides — structural fields such as `id`,
`parent_id`, and `status` always come from the occurrence or its parent.

rescheduled_from.description:
Set on the occurrence that lands on the target date after a
reschedule, carrying the origin date the occurrence moved from.
Pairs with `rescheduled_to` on the origin date so a client can
render a "moved from" affordance.

rescheduled_to.description:
Set on the origin occurrence after a reschedule: the origin date's
record is marked dropped and points to the new date the occurrence
moved to. Pairs with `rescheduled_from` on the target date so a
client can render a "moved to" affordance.

tz.description:
IANA timezone the client is operating in (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles").
Resolves the default day so the mutation lands on the user's local
calendar date rather than UTC. Optional: when absent the backend falls
back to the user's persisted timezone, then UTC.

PresignBatchPayload.description:
Request body to presign a batch of attachment uploads. One entry per
file the client intends to upload; each returns a presigned PUT URL the
browser uploads to before commit.

Recurrence.description:
A recurrence rule: a repeating [`Cadence`] plus an optional [`RecurrenceEnd`]
bound.

On the wire the cadence fields are flattened to the top level, so
`{"type":"daily"}` is a valid recurrence, and a `Never` end emits no `end`
key (the `end` key appears only for `OnDate` / `AfterCount` bounds).

oneOf.description:
Inclusive of this whole LOCAL day. Serialized to a UTC `UNTIL` at the
local end-of-day so a user west of UTC keeps the last day.

RecurrenceEnd.description:
Optional upper bound on a recurrence. The default `Never` is an open-ended
recurrence ("the trash goes out every Tuesday," forever). `OnDate` and
`AfterCount` are mutually exclusive (RFC 5545 forbids `UNTIL` with `COUNT`);
they serialize to `UNTIL` / `COUNT`.

RescheduleOccurrencePayload.description:
Request body for rescheduling an occurrence to a new date. The
caller supplies the target date; the origin occurrence is marked
dropped (with `rescheduled_to` set to the new date) and a fresh
scheduled occurrence is created on the target date carrying
`rescheduled_from` set to the origin date.

SavedSearch.description:
A user's saved search — a named pointer to a `/tasks` URL query string.

Stored as a JSON-encoded `Vec<SavedSearch>` under
`user:{user_id}:saved_searches` (mirrors pinned-tasks storage).

query_string.description: Raw URL query string (without leading "?"), e.g. "kinds=task&statuses=open".

cascade_subtasks.description:
When `true`, marking the occurrence done or dropped also moves every
still-open subtask to the matching terminal status. When `false` (the
default), an occurrence that still has open subtasks is rejected
(HTTP 409).

When `true`, marking the occurrence done or dropped also moves every
still-open subtask to the matching terminal status. When `false` (the
default), an occurrence that still has open subtasks is rejected
(HTTP 409).

SetOccurrencePayload.description: Request body to mark a chore or habit occurrence done or dropped.

cascade_subtasks.description:
When `true`, marking the occurrence done or dropped also moves every
still-open subtask to the matching terminal status. When `false` (the
default), an occurrence that still has open subtasks is rejected
(HTTP 409).

oneOf.description:
The user has at least one Deferno-challengeable second factor; the
password-only session is stashed server-side (`mfa_pending`) and the
client must complete one of `factor_types` via the /challenge endpoints.
We do NOT complete the OIDC login here — that would bypass MFA.

attachment_ids.description:
Attachments to associate with the feedback. Each entry is either
a bare presigned-attachment id or an object pairing an id with an
optional caption stamped on the attachment at submit time.
Bare-id strings remain supported.

SubtaskTemplate.description:
One entry in a recurring parent's subtask template. The Vec position
in the parent's `subtask_template` field determines materialization
order; subtask Tasks preserve that order.

attachments.description: Attachments embedded on this task; empty when there are none.

comment.description: Embedded comment on this task; empty when there are none.

descendant_done.description:
Computed: number of non-deleted descendants that are done.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

descendant_total.description:
Computed: total number of non-deleted descendants.
Populated by the list endpoint; not persisted.

oneOf.description:
Provenance for tasks imported from external systems (Google Tasks,
Todoist, etc.). `None` for Deferno-native rows. See
`ExternalProvenance` for the contract.

finished_at.description:
Timestamp when the task entered a terminal state (Done or Dropped).
Set on Open→Done, Open→Dropped, and InProgress→Dropped. Preserved
across Done→Dropped (does not lose the original Done time).
Cleared on any transition back to Open / InProgress / InReview.
Used by the visibility-window filter and by `mood_history` (which
gates on `status == Done` before reading this).

group_id.description:
Optional group ownership. Always None today — reserved for the
future Groups subsystem (Spec 2). Handlers ignore this field.

occurrence_id.description:
Set when this task is a subtask materialized from a recurring item's
occurrence; `null` for ordinary tasks. Removed together with that
occurrence.

owner_org_id.description: Owning org. Defaults to `Uuid::nil()` for unmigrated records.

series_id.description:
ID of the defernodate Series backing a recurring sibling that shares
this Task's UUID. Tasks themselves are non-recurring (recurrence
lives on Habit/Chore/Event), but this pointer is preserved across
the Task ↔ sibling boundary so series-aware reads still resolve.

subtask_template_id.description:
Set when this task was materialized from a recurring item's subtask
template; holds that template entry's id. `null` for ordinary tasks
and for ad-hoc subtasks.

TaskSummary.description:
Lightweight task summary for list endpoints — omits description,
actions history, mood vectors, and other fields that are only
useful when viewing a single task in detail.

ThemeFamily.description:
Visual theme family. Deferno is the brand palette (beige); Mono is a
future grayscale palette.

ThemeMode.description:
Light/dark mode within a theme family. `Auto` follows the user's
system `prefers-color-scheme`. Default is `Light` — explicitly chosen
over `Auto` so a fresh user never lands on `Dark` from system pref alone.

TimelineComment.description:
An Activity-timeline comment: a stored [`Comment`] plus the
presentation-only date of the occurrence it was found on.

`occurrence_date` is `null` for task-level comments — which have no
occurrence — and set to the occurrence date for per-occurrence comments
on recurring items. On the wire it flattens to look like a plain comment:
for task-level comments the absent `occurrence_date` adds nothing, while
per-occurrence comments carry the extra date field.

TokenKind.description: Discriminates between user-facing API tokens and MCP session tokens.

UpdateAttachmentCaptionPayload.description:
Request body to set, change, or clear an attachment's caption. Omitting
`caption` is rejected (nothing to change); `null` clears the caption; a
string sets it after validation.

oneOf.description:
Optional replacement for how the chore's schedule advances. Omit to
leave it unchanged.

complete_by.description:
PATCH-style field. `Absent` = no-op, `Null` = rejected at validation
(required field), `Set(v)` = update.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`. Send a
value to set it, `null` to clear it, or omit to leave it unchanged.

recurrence_id.description:
Accepted for symmetry with task/event but unused when scope is "all"
(the only scope chores support today).

subtask_template.description:
Replaces the entire per-occurrence subtask checklist. Omit to leave
it unchanged.

UpdateCommentPayload.description:
Body for PATCH /comments/{comment_id}.
Either field may be None — sending both None is a no-op (422).

complete_by.description:
PATCH-style field. `Absent` = no-op, `Null` = rejected at validation
(required field), `Set(v)` = update.

recurrence_id.description:
ISO local datetime identifying which instance to modify. Required
when `recurring_scope` is "this" or "following".

recurring_scope.description:
For recurring events: "this" | "following" | "all". Ignored for
non-recurring events (no `series_id`).

subtask_template.description:
Replaces the entire per-occurrence subtask checklist. Omit to leave
it unchanged.

complete_by.description:
PATCH-style field. `Absent` = no-op, `Null` = rejected at validation
(required field), `Set(v)` = update.

deadline_time_of_day.description:
Per-occurrence deadline as a local time of day, `"HH:MM"`. Send a
value to set it, `null` to clear it, or omit to leave it unchanged.

recurrence_id.description:
Accepted for symmetry with task/event but unused when scope is "all"
(the only scope habits support today).

subtask_template.description:
Replaces the entire per-occurrence subtask checklist. Omit to leave
it unchanged.

recurrence_id.description: ISO local datetime identifying which instance to modify (required for "this" and "following").

caption.description: Optional caption stamped on the attachment at commit time.

dashboard_done_visibility_seconds.description: Same idea, applied client-side on the Dashboard.

drag_and_drop_enabled.description:
Experimental: when true, the webui renders the task-card drag handle
and the dnd-kit reorder/reparent surface. Default `false` because
the surface is still maturing and we don't want to expose it to
users by default. Backward-compat: pre-shipping blobs lack the key,
so `serde(default = "...")` populates `false` instead of failing.

global_done_visibility_seconds.description:
Seconds past `finished_at` (tasks) or `complete_by` (one-off events)
after which a non-recurring item is hidden from default lists.
`None` ⇒ never hide.

theme_family.description:
Visual theme family. `serde(default)` + lenient deserializer so a
missing or unknown stored value does not poison the whole blob.

time_zone.description:
IANA timezone name (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles"). `None` ⇒ UTC.
Used to compute "today" / day windows for daily-plan and calendar
endpoints. Per-request `?tz=` query parameter overrides this.

tracking_enabled.description:
Whether the webui captures behavioural analytics for this user.
Default `true` (opt-in). Backward-compat: blobs from before this
field shipped have no key, so `serde(default = "...")` populates
`true` rather than failing.

UserSettingsPatch.description:
Partial update DTO. `None` on a field ⇒ "leave unchanged".
`Some(None)` ⇒ "set this field to null". `Some(Some(n))` ⇒ "set to n".

Theme fields are simple `Option<T>` — they aren't nullable, so the
double-Option discriminator isn't needed.

drag_and_drop_enabled.description:
Experimental drag-and-drop kill-switch. Same two-state semantics
as `tracking_enabled`: `None` ⇒ leave unchanged.

tracking_enabled.description:
Two-state preference; no "clear-to-null" semantic, so a plain
`Option<bool>` is sufficient — `None` means "leave unchanged".

bearer_token.description: Personal API token issued by `POST /auth/tokens`. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. This is the path native mobile clients use; bearer requests skip the cookie CSRF guard.

session_cookie.name: id
session_cookie.description: Browser session cookie set by the web sign-in flow (axum-login). Mutations additionally require the `X-Requested-With: deferno` header.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Authentication, the current user, API tokens, and user settings.

tags.name: tasks
tags.description: Task CRUD, search, and list views.

tags.name: items
tags.description: Polymorphic work-item surface (Task/Habit/Chore/Event) addressed by id, ref, sequence, or alias.

tags.name: occurrences
tags.description: Per-date occurrences of habits, chores, and events: list, set status, delete, reschedule.

tags.name: comments
tags.description: Comments on tasks/items and on event occurrences.

tags.name: attachments
tags.description: Presign / commit / list / caption / delete attachments on tasks and event occurrences.

tags.name: plan
tags.description: The daily plan, today's tasks, the calendar feed, and mood history.

tags.name: pinned
tags.description: Pinned tasks/items shown in the sidebar.
